channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Puerto Rico swears in new governor days after major blackout hit island
item.description: Jenniffer González Colón has been sworn in as Puerto Rico’s new governor as the island prepared for a normally ebullient ceremony overshadowed by widespread anger over a blackout that hit the U.S. territory days ago.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Impeached South Korean president issues defiant message as he faces possible detention
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol, in a message to conservative supporters rallying outside his Seoul residence, vowed to “fight to the end” against “anti-state forces,” while his legal team warned Thursday that police officers attempting to detain him could fa
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Montenegro mourns after gunman kills at least 12 people before shooting himself
item.description: Shock and dismay prevails in Montenegro after a gunman fatally shot 12 people including two children in a western town before killing himself.
item.dc:creator: Predrag Milic, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill at least 18 in Gaza, including 3 children, officials say
item.description: Palestinian and hospital officials say Israeli airstrikes killed at least 18 people in the Gaza Strip, including three children and two high-ranking officers in the Hamas-run police force.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bangladesh court again rejects bail for Hindu leader who led rallies
item.description: A court in southeastern Bangladesh has rejected a plea for bail by a jailed Hindu leader who led large rallies in the Muslim-majority country demanding better security for minority groups.
item.dc:creator: Julhas Alam, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, WGN 9

item.title: Power is restored to nearly all of Puerto Rico after a major blackout
item.description: BAYAMÓN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Power was restored to nearly all electrical customers across Puerto Rico on Wednesday after a sweeping blackout plunged the U.S. territory into darkness on New Year’s Eve.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA PÉREZ PINTADO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An armed man kills at least 10 people, including 2 children, in a shooting rampage in Montenegro
item.description: Montenegro’s interior minister says at least 10 people, including two children, were killed in a shooting in the western city of Cetinje.
item.dc:creator: Predrag Milic, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protesters in Syria demand justice for disappeared activists and accountability from all factions
item.description: Protesters in Syria have held a sit-in demanding justice for four activists who were forcibly disappeared in 2013 and whose fate remains one of the most haunting mysteries of the country’s 13-year civil war.
item.dc:creator: Omar Albam And Sally Abou Aljoud, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope calls for protection of life in New Year's message
item.description: Pope Francis called for the protection of life at New Year’s Day Mass on Wednesday, offering a seemingly anti-abortion message. “Let us entrust this new year to Mary, Mother of God,” Francis said in his homily at Saint Peter’s Basilica.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: South Korea air crash investigators extract black box data as grieving families mourn the victims
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Grieving relatives of the victims of the South Korea plane crash gathered at the site to pay respects to their loved ones on New Year’s Day, as officials said they've extracted data from one of the retrieved black boxes to find the exact cause of the crash.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lemon calls Trump supporters 'f‑‑‑ing idiots' amid H-1B visa infighting
item.description: Former CNN host Don Lemon poked fun at the Republican Party's infighting over H-1B visas — a key program to attract international talent that’s been criticized as too complicated and susceptible to abuse — calling supporters of President-elect Trump "dumb f‑‑‑ing idiots.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Russia, Ukraine swap more than 300 prisoners
item.description: Russia and Ukraine announced Monday a swap of more than 300 prisoners of war. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced his team secured the return of 189 Ukrainians, while Russia announced the return of 150 service members.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Hundreds of soldiers freed in the latest prisoner exchange between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia and Ukraine have exchanged prisoners of war in the latest such swap that saw the release of hundreds of captives and was brokered with the help of the United Arab Emirates, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taking back Panama Canal would require war: Former ambassador
item.description: Former U.S. Ambassador to Panama John Feeley on Sunday said it would take a war for the United States to take back the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Uncovering the atrocities of the Assad regime at its 'death factory' on the hill
item.description: Sednaya prison was the site where thousands of Syrians were tortured and killed under Assad’s regime. Investigators have uncovered mass graves, and are calling for justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu completes prostate surgery after UTI diagnosis
item.description: The prostate surgery that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu underwent on Sunday has been completed, Fox News has learned. The Israeli leader suffered a UTI before the procedure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea plane's final moments captured on video before hitting concrete barrier, triggering explosion
item.description: A video released Sunday shows the exact moment a plane skidded off a runway in South Korea before crashing into a concrete barrier, causing an explosion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu to undergo major surgery after UTI diagnosis
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will undergo prostate removal surgery on Sunday amid multiple conflicts across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plane skids off runway in South Korea, killing at least 177
item.description: A passenger plane burst into flames Sunday after it skidded off a runway at a South Korean airport and slammed into a concrete fence when its front landing gear apparently failed to deploy.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Putin apologizes for 'tragic incident' but stops short of saying Azerbaijani plane was shot down
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday apologized to his Azerbaijani counterpart for what he called a “tragic incident” following the crash of an Azerbaijani airliner in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people, but stopped short of acknowledging that Moscow was responsible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
